html:
[4849]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 服五味子 - Literal phrase meaning 'to consume the five-flavored berry,' referring to the practice of taking a specific herb for health benefits.
  • 移门子食服五味子六十年色如玉女入水不霑入火不灼 - Hyperbolic description of the effects of consuming the five-flavored berry over sixty years, suggesting that the person's appearance becomes like that of a jade maiden, and they become impervious to water and fire.
  • 食术 - Literal phrase meaning 'to eat the aloe plant,' referring to the practice of consuming aloe for medicinal purposes.
  • 南阳文氏説其先祖汉末大乱逃壶山中饥困欲死有一人教之食术云遂不饥十年乃来还乡里顔色更少气力胜故自説在山中时身轻欲跳登髙履险歴日不倦行氷雪中了不知寒常见一髙岩上有数人对博戏者有读书者俛而视之文氏因闻其相问言此子可呼上否其一人答未可也林子明服术十一年耳长五寸身轻如飞能超逾渊谷二丈许 - Detailed narrative of the effects of eating aloe, including physical transformations and supernatural abilities.
  • 服桃胶 - Literal phrase meaning 'to consume peach gum,' referring to the practice of taking a specific substance for health benefits.
  • 桃胶以桑木灰渍服之百病愈久久身有光在晦夜之地如月出也多服之则可以断谷矣 - Description of the effects of consuming peach gum, suggesting that it can cure various ailments, give the body a radiant glow, and eventually allow one to survive without food.
  • 服地黄 - Literal phrase meaning 'to consume rehmannia root,' referring to the practice of taking a specific herb for health benefits.
  • 楚子服地黄八年夜视有光手上车弩 - Description of the effects of consuming rehmannia root, suggesting that it can enhance night vision and strength.
  • 服逺志 - Literal phrase meaning 'to consume polygala root,' referring to the practice of taking a specific herb for health benefits.
  • 陵阳子仲服逺志二十年有子二十七人开书所视不忘 - Description of the effects of consuming polygala root, suggesting that it can enhance memory and cognitive abilities.
  • 服天门冬 - Literal phrase meaning 'to consume asparagus root,' referring to the practice of taking a specific herb for health benefits.
  • 杜子微服天门冬八十年妾有子百四十人日行二百里 - Description of the effects of consuming asparagus root, suggesting that it can enhance fertility and physical endurance.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 五味子 - Traditional Chinese herb known for its health benefits.
  • 南阳文氏 - Refers to a family from Nanyang, a region in China.
  • 汉末大乱 - Refers to the end of the Han Dynasty, a period of political turmoil.
  • 壶山 - A mountain in China, possibly a fictional or symbolic location.
  • 桃胶 - Substance derived from peach trees, used in traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 桑木灰 - Refers to the ash of mulberry trees, used in traditional Chinese medicine.
  • 地黄 - Traditional Chinese herb known for its health benefits.
  • 逺志 - Traditional Chinese herb known for its health benefits.
  • 天门冬 - Traditional Chinese herb known for its health benefits.
  • 断谷 - Refers to the practice of fasting or not eating food.
  • 夜视有光 - Refers to enhanced night vision.
  • 超逾渊谷二丈许 - Refers to the ability to leap across deep valleys.

Proper Nouns

  • 五味子 - The five-flavored berry.
  • 南阳文氏 - A family from Nanyang.
  • 汉末大乱 - The end of the Han Dynasty.
  • 壶山 - A mountain in China.
  • 桃胶 - Peach gum.
  • 桑木灰 - Ash of mulberry trees.
  • 地黄 - Rehmannia root.
  • 逺志 - Polygala root.
  • 天门冬 - Asparagus root.
  • 断谷 - Fasting.
  • 夜视有光 - Enhanced night vision.
  • 超逾渊谷二丈许 - Leaping across deep valleys.

[4850]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 饮菊潭水 - Literally 'drinking water from the chrysanthemum pond,' used to describe the act of consuming water from a specific source known for its health benefits.
  • 其源傍芳菊被涯澳 - A descriptive phrase meaning 'its source is surrounded by fragrant chrysanthemums along the banks,' highlighting the natural setting of the pond.
  • 其滋液极甘 - Means 'its water is extremely sweet,' emphasizing the quality of the water.
  • 深谷中有三十余家 - Refers to 'over thirty households in the deep valley,' indicating the number of people living near the pond.
  • 不得穿井仰饮此水 - Means 'they cannot dig wells and must drink directly from the water,' suggesting the reliance on the natural spring.
  • 上夀二三百中夀百余 - Refers to 'those who live to be two or three hundred years old, and those who live to be over a hundred,' indicating the extraordinary longevity associated with drinking the water.
  • 其七十八十犹以为夭 - Means 'even those who live to seventy or eighty are considered short-lived,' further emphasizing the exceptional longevity.
  • 饮甘菊谷水 - Another phrase for 'drinking water from the sweet chrysanthemum valley,' similar to the first.
  • 南阳郦县山中有甘谷水 - Refers to 'in the mountains of Lishi County, Nanyang, there is sweet water in the valley,' specifying the location.
  • 所以甘者谷上左右皆生甘菊 - Means 'the water is sweet because the valley is surrounded by sweet chrysanthemums,' explaining the source of the water's sweetness.
  • 其临此谷中居民皆不穿井悉饮甘谷水 - Refers to 'all residents in the valley drink the sweet water from the valley, not digging wells,' indicating their reliance on the natural spring.
  • 无夭年人得此菊力也 - Means 'there are no short-lived people because of the power of the chrysanthemums,' attributing the longevity to the chrysanthemums.
  • 每到官常使郦县月送甘谷水四十斛以为饮食 - Means 'each time they took office, they would order Lishi County to send forty shi of sweet water monthly for their diet,' indicating the importance of the water.
  • 此诸公多患风痹及冒皆得愈 - Refers to 'these officials often suffered from wind arthralgia and other ailments, but all were cured,' highlighting the healing properties of the water.
  • 但不能大得其益如甘谷上居民生小便饮食此水者耳 - Means 'but they could not benefit as much as the residents in the valley who drink the water daily,' comparing the benefits of regular consumption.
  • 又菊花与薏花相似直以甘苦别之耳 - Refers to 'chrysanthemums and coix flowers are similar, distinguished only by their sweetness and bitterness,' explaining the difference between the two.
  • 今所在有贡菊但为少耳 - Means 'today, there are contributions of chrysanthemums everywhere, but they are few,' indicating the rarity of the chrysanthemums.
  • 率多生于水侧也 - Refers to 'they mostly grow near water,' describing the natural habitat of the chrysanthemums.
  • 缑氏山与郦县最多 - Means 'the most are found in the mountains of Gou and Lishi County,' specifying the locations with the highest concentration.
  • 仙方所谓白精更生周盈皆一菊而根茎花实异名其说甚美而近来服之者略无效 - Refers to 'the immortal formulas call it Baijing, Gengsheng, and Zhouying, all referring to the same chrysanthemum but with different names for its roots, stems, flowers, and fruits. The story is beautiful, but recently those who take it have seen little effect,' explaining the historical and current perceptions of the chrysanthemum.
  • 夫甘菊谷水南方气味亦未足言而其上居民以延年况得服好药安得无益乎 - Means 'the sweet chrysanthemum water from the south may not be as potent, but the residents in the valley live longer, and if they take good medicine, how can it not be beneficial?' concluding the passage with a rhetorical question.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 荆州菊潭 - Refers to 'the chrysanthemum pond in Jingzhou,' a specific location known for its health-giving water.
  • 南阳郦县 - Refers to 'Lishi County in Nanyang,' a specific location known for its sweet water.
  • 抱朴子 - Refers to 'Baopu Zi,' a famous alchemical and philosophical text by Ge Hong.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '饮菊潭水' is a simple statement but sets the tone for the entire passage.
  • The phrase '其源傍芳菊被涯澳其滋液极甘' is descriptive and should be read as a single unit.
  • The clause '深谷中有三十余家不得穿井仰饮此水' explains the living conditions of the people in the valley.
  • The clause '上夀二三百中夀百余其七十八十犹以为夭' contrasts the extraordinary longevity with the average lifespan.
  • The clause '菊能轻身益气令人久寿有征' summarizes the benefits of the water.
  • The phrase '饮甘菊谷水' is another way of referring to the same act of drinking the water.
  • The clause '南阳郦县山中有甘谷水' specifies the location of the sweet water.
  • The clause '所以甘者谷上左右皆生甘菊' explains the source of the water's sweetness.
  • The clause '其临此谷中居民皆不穿井悉饮甘谷水' describes the lifestyle of the residents.
  • The clause '饮者无不考寿高者百四五十岁下者不失八九十' emphasizes the longevity of the residents.
  • The clause '无夭年人得此菊力也' attributes the longevity to the chrysanthemums.
  • The clause '故司空王畅太尉刘宽太傅隗皆为南阳太守' lists notable officials who served in the region.
  • The clause '每到官常使郦县月送甘谷水四十斛以为饮食' describes the practice of the officials.
  • The clause '此诸公多患风痹及冒皆得愈' highlights the healing properties of the water.
  • The clause '但不能大得其益如甘谷上居民生小便饮食此水者耳' compares the benefits of regular consumption.
  • The clause '又菊花与薏花相似直以甘苦别之耳' explains the difference between chrysanthemums and coix flowers.
  • The clause '今所在有贡菊但为少耳' indicates the rarity of the chrysanthemums.
  • The clause '率多生于水侧也' describes the natural habitat of the chrysanthemums.
  • The clause '缑氏山与郦县最多' specifies the locations with the highest concentration of chrysanthemums.
  • The clause '仙方所谓白精更生周盈皆一菊而根茎花实异名其说甚美而近来服之者略无效' explains the historical and current perceptions of the chrysanthemum.
  • The clause '夫甘菊谷水南方气味亦未足言而其上居民以延年况得服好药安得无益乎' concludes the passage with a rhetorical question.

Proper Nouns

  • 荆州 - The ancient Chinese region of Jingzhou.
  • 南阳 - The ancient Chinese region of Nanyang.
  • 郦县 - The ancient county of Lishi.
  • 司空 - A high-ranking official position.
  • 太尉 - A high-ranking military official.
  • 太傅 - A high-ranking educational official.
  • 王畅 - The name of a notable official.

[4851]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 食黄精 - Literal phrase meaning 'to eat huángjīng,' referring to the practice of consuming the herb huángjīng (Polygonatum) for medicinal or longevity purposes.
  • 临川有士人 - Literal phrase meaning 'there was a scholar in Linchuan,' where Linchuan is a place name.
  • 虐遇其所使婢 - Literal phrase meaning 'mistreated the maid he employed,' where '虐遇' means 'cruelly treated.'
  • 婢不堪其毒 - Literal phrase meaning 'the maid could not bear his cruelty,' where '毒' refers to cruelty or harshness.
  • 即拔取濯水中 - Literal phrase meaning 'immediately pulled it up and washed it in the water,' where '濯' means 'to wash.'
  • 连根食之 - Literal phrase meaning 'ate it with the root,' indicating the entire plant was consumed.
  • 自是恒食 - Literal phrase meaning 'from then on, he ate it constantly,' where '恒' means 'constantly' or 'always.'
  • 遂不饥而更轻健 - Literal phrase meaning 'and he no longer felt hungry, becoming even lighter and stronger,' where '遂' means 'therefore' or 'then.'
  • 夜息大下 - Literal phrase meaning 'at night, resting under a large tree,' where '' is a variant of '树' (tree).
  • 闻草中兽走 - Literal phrase meaning 'he heard animals running in the grass,' where '走' means 'to run.'
  • 以为虎而惧 - Literal phrase meaning 'thinking it was a tiger and being afraid,' where '以为' means 'thinking' or 'believing.'
  • 因念得上杪乃佳也 - Literal phrase meaning 'thinking that reaching the top of the tree would be better,' where '杪' means 'top' or 'tip.'
  • 正尔念之而身已在杪矣 - Literal phrase meaning 'just as he thought about it, his body was already at the top of the tree,' where '正尔' means 'just then' or 'immediately.'
  • 及晓又念当下平地 - Literal phrase meaning 'by morning, he thought of returning to the ground,' where '及晓' means 'by morning.'
  • 又歘然而下 - Literal phrase meaning 'and suddenly descended,' where '歘然' means 'suddenly' or 'swiftly.'
  • 自是意有所之 - Literal phrase meaning 'from then on, whenever he had a thought,' where '意有所之' means 'having a thought of going somewhere.'
  • 身即飘然而去 - Literal phrase meaning 'his body would instantly fly away,' where '飘然' means 'floating' or 'suddenly.'
  • 或自一峰之一峰顶飞鸟焉 - Literal phrase meaning 'sometimes flying from one peak to another like a bird,' where '' is a variant of '如' (like).
  • 数岁 - Literal phrase meaning 'for several years,' where '数' means 'several' or 'many.'
  • 其家人伐薪见之 - Literal phrase meaning 'his family members who were cutting wood saw him,' where '伐薪' means 'cutting wood.'
  • 以吿其主 - Literal phrase meaning 'and told his master,' where '吿' is a variant of '告' (to inform).
  • 使捕之不得 - Literal phrase meaning 'and tried to capture him but failed,' where '捕' means 'to capture.'
  • 一日遇其在絶壁下 - Literal phrase meaning 'one day, they found him at the base of a sheer cliff,' where '絶壁' means 'sheer cliff.'
  • 即以细绳三面围之 - Literal phrase meaning 'immediately surrounded him with fine ropes on three sides,' where '三面' means 'three sides.'
  • 俄腾上山顶 - Literal phrase meaning 'and suddenly rose to the top of the mountain,' where '俄' means 'suddenly.'
  • 其主益骇异 - Literal phrase meaning 'his master became even more amazed,' where '骇异' means 'amazed' or 'astonished.'
  • 必欲致之 - Literal phrase meaning 'determined to bring him back,' where '致' means 'to bring' or 'to obtain.'
  • 或曰此婢也 - Literal phrase meaning 'someone said this was the maid,' where '或' means 'someone' or 'some people.'
  • 安有仙骨 - Literal phrase meaning 'how could she have the bones of a sage,' where '仙骨' means 'bones of a sage' or 'immortal bones.'
  • 不过得灵药饵之耳 - Literal phrase meaning 'it must be that she had eaten some magical medicine,' where '饵' means 'to eat' or 'to take as medicine.'
  • 试以盛多其五味 - Literal phrase meaning 'they tried offering a rich dish with many flavors,' where '盛' means 'rich dish' and '五味' means 'five flavors.'
  • 令甚香美 - Literal phrase meaning 'making it very fragrant and delicious,' where '香美' means 'fragrant and delicious.'
  • 值其往来之路 - Literal phrase meaning 'on the path he frequently traveled,' where '值' means 'on' or 'at.'
  • 观其食之否 - Literal phrase meaning 'to see if he would eat it,' where '观' means 'to see' or 'to observe.'
  • 如其言果来就食 - Literal phrase meaning 'as they said, he came to eat,' where '果' means 'indeed' or 'really.'
  • 遂为所擒 - Literal phrase meaning 'and was captured,' where '擒' means 'to capture.'
  • 具述其故 - Literal phrase meaning 'he told the full story,' where '具' means 'fully' or 'completely.'
  • 问其所食草之形 - Literal phrase meaning 'they asked about the shape of the grass he had eaten,' where '形' means 'shape' or 'form.'
  • 即黄精也 - Literal phrase meaning 'it was huángjīng,' where '即' means 'is' or 'it is.'
  • 复使之遂不能得其婢 - Literal phrase meaning 'they tried to find her again but could not,' where '复' means 'again' or 'once more.'
  • 数年亦卒 - Literal phrase meaning 'after several years, he also died,' where '卒' means 'died' or 'passed away.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 临川 - A place name, referring to the region of Linchuan in modern-day Jiangxi Province, China.
  • 士人 - Refers to a scholar or a person of learning, typically a literatus or a Confucian scholar.
  • 黄精 - A traditional Chinese herb, Polygonatum, known for its medicinal properties and believed to promote longevity.
  • 仙骨 - Refers to the bones of a sage or immortal, symbolizing spiritual or supernatural qualities.
  • 灵药 - Refers to magical or miraculous medicine, often associated with immortality or extraordinary abilities.
  • 稽神录 - The title of a collection of supernatural stories and legends, compiled by Gan Bao during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 临川 - Place name, referring to the region of Linchuan in modern-day Jiangxi Province, China.
  • 黄精 - Traditional Chinese herb, Polygonatum.
  • 稽神录 - Title of a collection of supernatural stories and legends, compiled by Gan Bao during the Eastern Jin Dynasty.

[4852]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太平广记 - A classical collection of stories and anecdotes, often used to refer to a specific volume or section.
  • 钦定四库全书 - A massive compilation of Chinese literature commissioned by the Qing dynasty.
  • 草木十 - A category within the '太平广记' that deals with plants and trees, specifically the tenth part.
  • 木怪 - A general term for supernatural or anomalous phenomena associated with trees.
  • 留其使家居买田 - Means 'he left his officials at home to buy land.'
  • 田中有大十余围 - Describes a large tree with a circumference of more than ten feet.
  • 扶疎葢数亩 - Means 'with a spreading canopy covering several acres.'
  • 赤汁六七斗出 - Refers to a red liquid (blood) that flowed out in several buckets.
  • 司空辟髙为侍御史 - Means 'the Grand Works commissioned Zhang Liao as an Imperial Censor.'
  • 兖州刺史 - Refers to the Governor of Yan State.
  • 荐祝祖考 - Means 'to offer sacrifices to ancestors.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太平广记 - A comprehensive collection of stories and anecdotes compiled during the Song dynasty, often used to illustrate moral lessons or supernatural events.
  • 钦定四库全书 - A monumental project commissioned by Emperor Qianlong of the Qing dynasty, which aimed to preserve and organize Chinese literature.
  • 桂阳 - An ancient county in present-day Hunan Province, known for its strategic importance.
  • 江夏 - An ancient county in present-day Hubei Province, historically significant for its role in various dynasties.
  • 司空 - One of the Three Excellencies in the Han dynasty, responsible for civil engineering and public works.
  • 侍御史 - A high-ranking official in the imperial court, responsible for oversight and inspection.
  • 兖州 - An ancient state in northern China, now part of Shandong Province.
  • 荐祝祖考 - A ritual practice of offering sacrifices to ancestors, emphasizing filial piety and respect for the deceased.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '桂阳太守江夏张辽字叔高留其使家居买田' is elliptical and requires careful parsing to ensure clarity. It means that Zhang Liao, the Governor of Guiyang, left his officials at home to buy land.
  • The sentence '田中有大十余围扶疎葢数亩地不生谷' describes a large tree with a spreading canopy that covers several acres, and the land around it does not produce crops.
  • The sentence '赤汁六七斗出客惊怖归具白叔高' describes the event where red liquid (blood) flowed out in several buckets, causing the guest to be terrified and return to report to Zhang Liao.
  • The question '老赤汁有何等血' is rhetorical and emphasizes the unusual nature of the blood.
  • The sentence '是岁司空辟髙为侍御史兖州刺史以居二千石之尊过乡里荐祝祖考竟无他怪' is complex and requires breaking down into manageable parts to ensure clarity. It means that in that year, the Grand Works commissioned Zhang Liao as an Imperial Censor, and the Governor of Yan State elevated him to a position of great honor. He returned to his hometown to offer sacrifices to his ancestors, and no other strange events occurred.

Proper Nouns

  • 太平广记 - The title of the collection.
  • 钦定四库全书 - The title of the compilation.
  • 草木十 - The specific category within the collection.
  • 木怪 - The general term for the phenomena described.
  • 桂阳 - The place name.
  • 江夏 - The place name.
  • 张辽 - The proper name of the governor.
  • 司空 - The title of the Grand Works.
  • 侍御史 - The title of the Imperial Censor.
  • 兖州 - The place name.
  • 二千石 - A title indicating a high-ranking official.

[4853]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 建安郡太守 - Title meaning 'Governor of Jian'an Commandery.'
  • 伐大樟 - '伐' means to cut down, and '大樟' refers to a large camphor tree.
  • 不数斧有血出 - '不数斧' means 'after a few blows,' and '有血出' means 'blood appeared.'
  • 断有物人面狗身从中出 - '断' means 'cut,' '有物' means 'an object,' '人面狗身' means 'human face and dog body,' and '从中出' means 'came out from the tree.'
  • 此名彭侯 - '此名' means 'this is called,' and '彭侯' is a name.
  • 乃烹食之 - '乃' means 'then,' and '烹食之' means 'cooked and ate it.'
  • 白泽图曰 - '白泽图' is a mythical book that records knowledge of spirits and supernatural creatures.
  • 木之精名彭侯状如黒狗无尾可烹食之 - '木之精' means 'spirit of the wood,' '名彭侯' means 'called Peng Hou,' '状如黒狗无尾' means 'looks like a black dog without a tail,' and '可烹食之' means 'can be cooked and eaten.'
  • 吴聂友字文悌豫章新涂人 - '吴聂友' is a person's name, '字文悌' is his courtesy name, and '豫章新涂人' means 'from Xintu, Yuzhang.'
  • 少时贫贱常好射猎 - '少时' means 'when young,' '贫贱' means 'poor and lowly,' and '常好射猎' means 'often enjoyed hunting.'
  • 见一白鹿射之中寻踪血尽不知所在 - '见一白鹿' means 'saw a white deer,' '射之中' means 'shot and hit it,' '寻踪' means 'followed the trail,' '血尽' means 'blood ran out,' and '不知所在' means 'did not know where it was.'
  • 饥困卧梓下仰见所射鹿箭着枝怪之 - '饥困' means 'hungry and exhausted,' '卧梓下' means 'lay under a paulownia tree,' '仰见' means 'looked up and saw,' '所射鹿' means 'the deer he shot,' '箭着枝' means 'the arrow stuck in the tree branch,' and '怪之' means 'found it strange.'
  • 于是还家赍粮命子弟持斧伐之有血遂截为二板 - '于是' means 'then,' '还家' means 'returned home,' '赍粮' means 'took provisions,' '命子弟' means 'ordered his children,' '持斧伐之' means 'to take an axe and cut it down,' '有血' means 'blood appeared on the tree,' and '遂截为二板' means 'cut it into two boards.'
  • 牵置陂中常沉时复浮出 - '牵置' means 'dragged and placed,' '陂中' means 'in a pond,' '常沉' means 'often sank,' and '时复浮出' means 'sometimes floated up.'
  • 出家必有言友欲迎賔客常乗此版或于中流欲没客大惧友呵之复浮 - '出家' means 'left home,' '必有言' means 'always had something to say,' '友欲迎賔客' means 'he wanted to welcome guests,' '常乗此版' means 'often rode on this board,' '或于中流欲没' means 'sometimes almost sank in the middle of the stream,' '客大惧' means 'guests were very afraid,' '友呵之' means 'he shouted at it,' and '复浮' means 'it floated up again.'
  • 仕官如愿位至丹阳太守 - '仕官' means 'served as an official,' '如愿' means 'as he wished,' '位至' means 'reached the position of,' and '丹阳太守' means 'Governor of Danyang Commandery.'

  • 昔桐栢真人敷扬道义许谧茅容乗龙各赠制龙石十觔 - Phrase meaning 'In the past, the real person from Tongbai spread the teachings of righteousness and gave ten pounds of dragon stone to Xu Mi and Mao Rong, who rode dragons.'
  • 今亦应在请访之 - Phrase meaning 'Now it should also be there, please inquire about it.'
  • 帝勅命求之于茅山华阳隐居陶景得石两片 - Phrase meaning 'The emperor ordered to seek it from Mount Mao and the hidden residence of Huayang, Tao Hongjing found two pieces of stone.'
  • 是矣 - Phrase meaning 'This is it.'
  • 帝勅百工以于阗舒河中美玉造小函二以桐木灰发其光取宣州空青汰其甚精者用海鱼胶之成二缶 - Phrase meaning 'The emperor ordered the craftsmen to make two small boxes with beautiful jade from Yutian, Shuhe, and use the ash of the tallow tree to reveal its light, take the very fine turquoise from Xuanzhou, and use sea fish glue to make two cans.'

  • 乃以烧燕百事赂之令其通问以其上上者献龙女 - Phrase meaning 'They then used 100 burned swallows as a bribe to make it pass a message, and offered the very best to the dragon girl.'
  • 龙女食之大嘉 - Phrase meaning 'The dragon girl ate it and greatly praised it.'
  • 又上玉函青缶具陈帝防 - Phrase meaning 'They also presented the jade box and green can to the emperor.'
  • 洞中有千岁龙能变化出入人间有善译时俗之言 - Phrase meaning 'There is a thousand-year-old dragon in the cave that can change and enter and exit the human world, and is good at translating the language of the times.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 之以大珠三小珠七雜珠一石以报帝命 - Describes the gift given to the emperor: three large pearls, seven small pearls, one mixed pearl, and a stone. The structure is somewhat elliptical and requires careful translation to convey the full meaning.
  • 子春乗龙载珠还国 - Describes Zi Chun riding a dragon and carrying pearls back to his country. The imagery of riding a dragon is a common motif in Chinese mythology, often symbolizing divine or supernatural travel.
  • 食顷之间便至 - Means 'within a meal's time' or 'very quickly,' indicating a rapid journey.
  • 龙辞去 - Means 'the dragon departed,' signaling the end of the supernatural journey.
  • 荐珠帝大喜 - Means 'the emperor greatly rejoiced when the pearls were presented,' emphasizing the emperor's delight.
  • 得聘通灵异获天人之寳 - Means 'obtained a connection with the divine and received a heavenly treasure,' highlighting the mystical significance of the pearls.
  • 以珠示杰公 - Means 'he showed the pearls to Jie Gong,' indicating a specific person to whom the pearls were presented.
  • 杰公曰 - Marks the beginning of Jie Gong's commentary.
  • 三珠其一是天帝如意珠之下者 - Means 'three pearls, one of which is the lesser version of the Heavenly Emperor's Wishing Pearl.' The term '如意珠' (Wishing Pearl) is a metaphor for a powerful and valuable object.
  • 其二是骊龙珠之中者 - Means 'the second is the middle-grade pearl of the Black Dragon.'
  • 七珠二是虫珠五是海蚌珠人间之上者 - Means 'seven pearls, two of which are insect pearls, and five are superior pearls from sea clams.' The term '虫珠' (insect pearls) is less clear and may refer to pearls formed by insects.
  • 杂珠是蚌蛤等珠不如大珠之贵 - Means 'the mixed pearl is a pearl from clams or other mollusks, not as valuable as the large pearls.'
  • 帝遍示百僚朝廷咸谓杰公虚诞莫不诘之 - Means 'the emperor showed the pearls to all the officials, and the court unanimously called Jie Gong's words false, questioning him.'
  • 杰公曰 - Marks the beginning of Jie Gong's further explanation.
  • 如意珠上上者夜光照四十余里中者十里下者一里光之所及无风雨雷电水火刀兵诸毒厉 - Describes the properties of the Wishing Pearl, with the highest grade illuminating over forty li at night, the middle grade ten li, and the lowest grade one li. The light is said to ward off all natural and man-made disasters.
  • 骊珠上者夜光百歩中者十歩下者一室光之所及无蛇虺豸之毒 - Describes the properties of the Black Dragon Pearl, with the highest grade illuminating one hundred steps, the middle grade ten steps, and the lowest grade one room. The light is said to ward off snake and insect venom.
  • 虫珠七色而多赤六足二目当其凹处有旧如鐡鼻 - Describes the properties of the insect pearls, which are seven-colored and mostly red, with six legs and two eyes, and a hard, iron-like navel.
  • 蚌珠五色皆有夜光及数尺 - Describes the properties of the sea clam pearls, which are five-colored and have a night glow that extends several feet.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 无瑕者为之上有瑕者为下 - Classifies pearls based on their quality; 'those without flaws are superior, those with flaws are inferior.'
  • 珠蚌五于时与月盈亏 - Describes the natural cycle of pearl formation; 'pearl oysters produce pearls in accordance with the waxing and waning of the moon.'
  • 虵珠所致 - Refers to the origin of pearls from snakes; 'caused by the snake.'
  • 隋侯哙参即其事也 - Refers to historical figures and events; 'the story of the Duke of Sui and Kuai Shen.'
  • 使其自适帝命杰公记虵二珠斗余杂珠散于殿前 - Complex sentence meaning 'let them adapt to the imperial command, and the official Jie records the two large pearls and the rest of the mixed pearls scattered before the palace.'
  • 取大黄虵各十数处布珠中间 - Means 'take ten large yellow snakes and ten dark-colored snakes, place them in several places among the pearls.'
  • 防其珠鸣舞徘徊虵防其珠盘曲宛转 - Describes the snakes protecting their pearls, with the pearls dancing and the snakes coiling gracefully.
  • 羣臣观者莫不叹服 - Means 'all the ministers and spectators were amazed and admired.'
  • 帝复出如意龙虫等珠光之逺近七八九数皆如杰公之言 - Detailed description of the emperor's further actions and the accuracy of the official Jie's words.
  • 子春在龙宫得食如花如药如膏如饴食之香美赍食至京师得人间风日乃坚如石不可咀咽 - Describes the food obtained by Zichun in the dragon palace, which was fragrant and delicious but became hard like stone when brought to the capital.
  • 帝令秘府藏之拜子春为奉车都尉二弟为奉朝请赐布帛各千匹追访公驰往不为龙害之由为用麻油和蜡以作照鱼衣乃身有蜡气故也 - Series of actions by the emperor, including storing the food in the secret treasury, appointing Zichun and his brothers, and explaining the reason for the lack of harm from the dragon.

  • 回诣元照愿陈力致效 - I returned to visit Yuanzhao and wished to offer my strength and serve; '回' (huí) means 'to return,' '诣' (yì) means 'to visit,' '元照' (yuán zhào) is a name, '愿陈力致效' (yuàn chén lì zhì xiào) means 'wishes to offer his strength and serve'
  • 三叟再为请元照因言前山当路不便往来却之可否 - The three old men again requested Yuanzhao, who then said that the front mountain blocking the road was inconvenient for travel, and asked whether to remove it or not; '三叟' (sān sǒu) means 'three old men,' '再为请' (zài wéi qǐng) means 'again requested,' '元照' (yuán zhào) is a name, '因言' (yīn yán) means 'then said,' '前山' (qián shān) means 'the front mountain,' '当路' (dāng lù) means 'blocking the road,' '不便往来' (bù biàn wǎng lái) means 'inconvenient for travel,' '却之可否' (què zhī kě fǒu) means 'whether to remove it or not'
  • 固是小事耳但勿以风雷为责即可为之 - It is indeed a small matter, but do not use wind and thunder as a responsibility, and it can be done; '固是' (gù shì) means 'indeed,' '小事耳' (xiǎo shì ěr) means 'a small matter,' '但勿以' (dàn wù yǐ) means 'but do not use,' '风雷' (fēng léi) means 'wind and thunder,' '为责' (wéi zé) means 'as a responsibility,' '即可为之' (jí kě wéi zhī) means 'can be done'
  • 是夕雷霆震击及晓开霁寺前豁然数里如掌 - That night, thunder and lightning struck, and by dawn, it cleared up. In front of the temple, several miles suddenly opened up like a palm; '是夕' (shì xī) means 'that night,' '雷霆震击' (léi tíng zhèn jī) means 'thunder and lightning struck,' '及晓' (jí xiǎo) means 'by dawn,' '开霁' (kāi jì) means 'cleared up,' '寺前' (sì qián) means 'in front of the temple,' '豁然' (huò rán) means 'suddenly opened up,' '数里' (shù lǐ) means 'several miles,' '如掌' (rú zhǎng) means 'like a palm'

  • 每遇水色可爱则遗剑环于水命摩诃取之以为戏乐 - Means 'every time he encountered beautiful water, he would throw the sword and ring into the water and order Mahā to retrieve them for fun.' Describes the playful activity.
  • 如是数载因渡巢湖亦投剑环而令取之 - Means 'for several years, and when crossing Chaohu Lake, he also threw the sword and ring into the water and ordered Mahā to retrieve them.' Extends the activity over time.
  • 摩诃才入获剑环而便出曰为毒虵所啮遽刄去一指乃能得免 - Means 'Mahā just entered the water, retrieved the sword and ring, and came out saying, 'I was bitten by a venomous snake, and had to cut off a finger to escape.' Describes the dangerous incident.
  • 焦遂曰摩诃所伤得非隂灵怒乎 - Means 'Jiao Shui said, 'Wasn't Mahā injured because of the anger of the hidden spirits?'' Raises a question about the cause of the injury.
  • 盖水府不欲人窥也 - Means 'It is because the water god does not want people to spy on him.' Provides a possible explanation.
  • 岘曰敬奉喻然某常慕谢康乐之为人云终当乐死山水但狥所好莫知其他 - Means 'Xian said, 'I understand your point, but I often admire the character of Xie Kangle, who said he would die happily in the mountains and waters, just following his preferences without knowing anything else.' Expresses a personal preference.
  • 且栖迟逆旅之中载于大块之上居布素之贱擅贵游之欢浪迹怡情仅三十载固亦分也 - Means 'And living in temporary lodging, riding on the vast earth, residing in humble conditions, enjoying the pleasures of high society, wandering and finding joy, for nearly thirty years, it is also my fate.' Reflects on the speaker's life.
  • 不得升玉墀见天子施功惠养逞志平生亦其分也 - Means 'Not being able to ascend the jade steps to see the emperor, to serve the people and fulfill my ambitions in life, is also my fate.' Expresses a sense of resignation.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 归舟忽大风云色陡暗寸歩黯黒 - Describes a sudden and dramatic change in weather, with the boat being engulfed in darkness. '寸歩黯黒' is an idiomatic expression meaning 'every step was in darkness,' emphasizing the intensity of the darkness.
  • 至平明观夜来饮所乃空林树而已 - '至平明' means 'until dawn,' and '空林树' refers to an empty forest with trees. The phrase '而已' means 'nothing more than,' indicating that the scene was nothing more than a forest.
  • 汉阳缆行至昨晚口江岸人家见十数人似有非常 - '汉阳缆' refers to the act of tying up the boat in Han Yang. '口江岸' is a specific location on the riverbank. '似有非常' means 'seemed to be something unusual.'
  • 江口溺杀四人至二更后却捞出三人已卒其一人虽似死而未甚有巫女以杨栁水洒拂禁呪乆之能言 - '江口溺杀四人' means 'four people were drowned at the river mouth.' '至二更后' means 'after the second watch of the night.' '却捞出三人已卒' means 'three were rescued but already dead.' '其一人虽似死而未甚' means 'one seemed dead but not quite.' '巫女以杨栁水洒拂禁呪乆之能言' describes a witch using willow water to sprinkle and perform a long spell, eventually allowing the person to speak.
  • 今在何处已发舟也汉阳乃念昨宵之事及感懐之什皆可验也 - '今在何处已发舟也' means 'where is he now, having already set sail?' '汉阳乃念昨宵之事及感懐之什' means 'Han Yang then recalled the events of last night and the poems of sentiment.' '皆可验也' means 'all can be verified.'
  • 知悉以人血为酒尔三日方平【出博异志】 - '知悉以人血为酒尔' means 'it was known that the wine was made from human blood.' '三日方平' means 'it took three days to calm down.' '【出博异志】' indicates the source of the text, *Bo Yi Zhi* (Book of Strange Stories).

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 因夷人卖奴年十四五 - This phrase is ambiguous. It could mean 'because a foreigner sold a boy aged 14 or 15' or 'because a boy aged 14 or 15 was sold by a foreigner.'
  • 令其沉潜虽经日移时终无所苦云 - This phrase is elliptical. It means 'he made him dive and stay underwater for long periods without any difficulty,' but the exact duration and context need to be specified.
  • 蜀之溪壑潭洞无不届也 - This phrase is a long sequence of nouns, which must be read as a single catalogue of places.
  • 邯因买之易其名曰水精 - This phrase is a two-part statement: 'Zhou Han bought him and renamed him 'Shuijing' (Water Gem).'
  • 异其能也 - This phrase is a simple statement of admiration, but the exact nature of the admiration needs to be clarified.
  • 邯自蜀乘舟下峡抵江陵 - This phrase is a sequence of actions: 'Zhou Han traveled by boat from Shu through the Three Gorges to Jiangling.'
  • 经瞿塘滟滪 - This phrase is a simple statement of passing through, but the exact context and significance need to be clarified.
  • 遂令水精沉而视其邃远 - This phrase is a two-part statement: 'he then made Shuijing dive and observe the deep and distant waters.'
  • 水精入移时而出多探金银器物 - This phrase is a two-part statement: 'Shuijing dived for a while and came out with many gold and silver objects.'
  • 邯喜甚 - This phrase is a simple statement of emotion, but the exact nature of the pleasure needs to be clarified.
  • 每航船于江潭皆令水精沉之复有所得 - This phrase is a two-part statement: 'every time they sailed on rivers and lakes, he made Shuijing dive and they found more treasures.'
  • 防流抵江都 - This phrase is a simple statement of reaching a destination, but the exact route and context need to be clarified.
  • 经牛渚矶古云最深处是温峤爇犀照水怪之濵 - This phrase is a two-part statement: 'they passed by Niu Zhi Rock, where it was said that the deepest part was where Wen Qiao burned a rhinoceros horn to illuminate water spirits.'
  • 又使没入移时复得宝玉云甚有水怪莫能名状皆怒目防手身仅免祸 - This phrase is a complex statement with multiple clauses: 'he made Shuijing dive again, and after a while, he found precious jewels. There were many water spirits, but he could not describe them; they had angry eyes and raised hands, barely escaping danger.'
  • 因兹邯亦至富赡 - This phrase is a simple statement of consequence, but the exact nature of the wealth needs to be clarified.
  • 后数年邯有友人王泽牧相州邯适河北而访之泽甚喜与之游宴日不能暇 - This phrase is a complex statement with multiple clauses: 'several years later, Zhou Han had a friend named Wang Ze, who was the governor of Xiangzhou. Zhou Han went to Hebei to visit him, and Wang Ze was very happy to see him and entertained him every day.'
  • 因相与至州北 - This phrase is a simple statement of movement, but the exact destination and context need to be clarified.

  • 天然盤石而甃成八角焉 - Describes the well's construction using naturally occurring stones to form an eight-sided structure.
  • 濶可三丈余 - Indicates the well's width, approximately three zhang (a traditional Chinese unit of length).
  • 旦暮烟云蓊鬱漫衍百余歩 - Describes the dense mist and clouds that surround the well during dawn and dusk, spreading over a hundred steps.
  • 晦夜有光如火红射出千尺鉴物若昼 - Describes the well emitting a red light at night that illuminates objects as if it were daytime.
  • 金龙潜其底 - Refers to a golden dragon supposedly hidden at the bottom of the well.
  • 亢阳祷之亦甚有应泽 - Means 'praying for drought relief has been very effective,' suggesting the well's mystical properties.
  • 邯笑曰甚易 - Indicates that Han (the character's name) laughs and says it's very easy.
  • 水精曰汝可与我投此井到底看有何怪异 - Refers to Shuijing (another character's name) being asked to dive into the well to see if there are any strange phenomena.
  • 泽亦当有所赏也 - Means 'Zé (another character's name) will also be rewarded.'
  • 水精已乆不入水忻然脱衣沉之 - Describes Shuijing, who hasn't been in water for a long time, happily undresses and dives into the well.
  • 良乆而出语邯曰有一黄龙极大鳞如金色抱数颗明珠熟寐 - After a long time, Shuijing comes out and tells Han that there is a large yellow dragon with golden scales sleeping and holding several pearls.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 诈洁俄而报盆所又有蛇者二子之盆所又欲击 - Highly elliptical phrase describing two sons about to attack something (likely a snake) in a place called '盆所' (a container or storage area). '诈洁' (zha jie) is unclear and may be a typo or mistranscription.
  • 腾空而去 - Literally means 'to fly away into the air,' but can also be interpreted as 'to disappear suddenly.'
  • 觇之则此山间风云暴起飞沙走石不瞬息至寺天地晦暝对面相失寺中人闻风云暴起中云莫错击须臾雨火中半下书生之宇并焚荡且尽 - Long, complex sentence describing a sudden and violent storm that causes chaos and destruction. '觇之' (chan zhi) means 'to spy on' or 'to observe,' '风云暴起' (feng yun bao qi) means 'a sudden storm of wind and clouds,' '飞沙走石' (fei sha zou shi) means 'flying sand and rolling stones,' '天地晦暝' (tian di hui ming) means 'the sky and earth are dark and gloomy,' '对面相失' (dui mian xiang shi) means 'unable to see each other face-to-face,' and '寺中人闻风云暴起中云莫错击' (si zhong ren wen feng yun bao qi zhong yun mo cuo ji) means 'people in the temple heard the sudden storm and were told not to make mistakes.' '须臾雨火中半下书生之宇并焚荡且尽' (xu yu yu huo zhong ban xia shu sheng zhi yu bing fen dang qie jin) means 'in a moment, rain and fire fell, and the scholar's house was burned and destroyed completely.'
  • 韦子于寺廊下无事故神化之理亦甚昭然 - Wei Zi, who was at the temple porch, did not experience any harm, and the principle of transformation (or divine intervention) was clearly evident. '韦子' (Wei Zi) is a proper name, '寺廊下' (si lang xia) means 'under the temple porch,' and '神化之理' (shen hua zhi li) means 'the principle of transformation' or 'divine intervention.'
  • 不能全为善但吐少善言则蛟龙之祸不及矣而况于常行善道哉 - One cannot be entirely good, but if one speaks a little good word, the calamity of the dragon will not reach them. '不能全为善' (bu neng quan wei shan) means 'cannot be entirely good,' '吐少善言' (tu shao shan yan) means 'speak a little good word,' and '蛟龙之祸不及矣' (jiao long zhi huo bu ji yi) means 'the calamity of the dragon will not reach them.' '而况于常行善道哉' (er huan yu chang xing shan dao zai) means 'how much more so for those who always follow the path of goodness.'
  • 其二子尸迨两日于寺门南隅下方索得斯乃韦自说至于好杀者足以为戒矣 - The bodies of the two sons were found after two days, and it concludes that those who enjoy killing should take this as a warning.   • The phrase '诈洁俄而报盆所又有蛇者二子之盆所又欲击' is highly elliptical and requires careful parsing. The most likely interpretation is that two sons are about to attack a snake in a place called '盆所' (a container or storage area).
  • The phrase '腾空而去' can be interpreted as 'to fly away into the air' or 'to disappear suddenly,' depending on the context.
  • The long, complex sentence '觇之则此山间风云暴起飞沙走石不瞬息至寺天地晦暝对面相失寺中人闻风云暴起中云莫错击须臾雨火中半下书生之宇并焚荡且尽' describes a sudden and violent storm that causes chaos and destruction. The sentence structure is intricate, and each clause must be carefully translated to maintain the flow and meaning.
  • The phrase '韦子于寺廊下无事故神化之理亦甚昭然' suggests that Wei Zi, who was at the temple porch, did not experience any harm, and the principle of transformation (or divine intervention) was clearly evident.
  • The phrase '不能全为善但吐少善言则蛟龙之祸不及矣而况于常行善道哉' means that one cannot be entirely good, but if one speaks a little good word, the calamity of the dragon will not reach them. The rhetorical structure is important to preserve.
  • The phrase '其二子尸迨两日于寺门南隅下方索得斯乃韦自说至于好杀者足以为戒矣' describes the discovery of the bodies of the two sons after two days, and it concludes that those who enjoy killing should take this as a warning.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 粥毕二子计之曰此僧且在我等何由下山 - Introduces the sons' dialogue and their concern about the monk.
  • 遂诈僧云井中有异使窥之细窥次 - Describes the sons' deceptive action and the monk's reaction.
  • 二子推僧堕井其僧即时化为虎 - Describes the violent action and the supernatural transformation.
  • 二子以巨石镇之而毙矣 - Describes the final action of crushing the tiger.
  • 二子遂取银皿下山近昏黒而遇一猎人于道旁 - Sets the next scene and introduces the hunter.
  • 张窝弓树上为棚而居语二子曰无触我机 - Describes the hunter's setup and his warning.
  • 兼谓二子曰去山下犹逺诸虎方暴何不且上棚来 - Describes the hunter's suggestion.
  • 二子悸怖遂攀縁而上 - Describes the sons' reaction and their action.
  • 忽三五十人过或僧或道或丈夫或妇女歌唫者戏舞者前至窝所 - Describes the sudden appearance of a group of people.
  • 众怒曰朝来被二贼杀我禅和方今追捕之 - Describes the crowd's reaction and their pursuit.
  • 又敢有人张我军遂发其机而去 - Describes the crowd's final action.
  • 二子并闻其説遂诘猎者曰此是伥被虎所食 - Describes the sons' final revelation.
